evaluations.query: Create a PR for my feature branch
expected_behavior: Runs pre-flight checks (branch protection, uncommitted changes)
Launches 3 parallel validation agents (security, tests, quality)
Creates PR with conventional title format: type(#issue): description
Includes summary, changes list, and test plan in PR body

evaluations.query: Create a quick draft PR without validation
expected_behavior: Skips parallel validation agents for faster draft creation
Pushes branch if not already on remote
Creates PR directly with gh pr create
Gathers context from git log and diff

evaluations.query: Create a PR that depends on PR #50
expected_behavior: Sets base branch to the dependency PR branch
Notes dependency in PR body with a link to the upstream PR
Runs pre-flight checks including upstream sync
Verifies branch is not main or dev

evaluations.query: Review the open PR on my branch
expected_behavior: Does NOT trigger create-pr workflow since this is a review request
Recognizes this is a review request, not PR creation
Suggests using the review-pr skill instead

evaluations.query: Create a PR for my new user profile feature linked to issue #456
expected_behavior: Formats title under 70 characters with conventional commit prefix like feat
Includes issue number in scope parentheses as feat(#456) in the title
Uses imperative mood for the description such as add, fix, or update
Selects the correct prefix type based on the nature of the change

evaluations.query: What should I name my branch for fixing issue #789 about a login race condition?
expected_behavior: Uses lowercase with hyphens pattern like fix/789-login-race-condition for the branch name
Includes the issue number in the branch name for automated extraction
Keeps the description to 3 to 5 words for concise identification
Uses type prefix matching conventional commit types like fix for bug fixes

evaluations.query: My PR has 1200 lines of changes including a refactor, feature, and config updates. Should I split it?
expected_behavior: Identifies 1200 lines as too large and recommends splitting into smaller PRs
Suggests separating infrastructure and config changes from feature code in different PRs
Recommends keeping each PR under 400 lines of meaningful change for effective review
Shows how to use stacked PRs when a feature requires more than 800 lines

evaluations.query: What sections should I include in my PR description for a new JWT auth feature?
expected_behavior: Includes required sections: Summary, Changes list, Test Plan, and issue reference
Uses Closes #N syntax to auto-close the linked issue on merge to main
Formats the body with HEREDOC pattern for correct multi-line formatting in gh CLI
Keeps Summary focused on the why rather than repeating what the diff shows

evaluations.query: Run all necessary checks before creating my PR for the auth feature branch.
expected_behavior: Verifies the current branch is not main or dev before allowing PR creation
Checks for a clean working tree with no uncommitted changes using git status
Runs lint, type check, and test suite locally before creating the PR
Scans the diff for secrets like API keys or tokens and blocks if found

evaluations.query: Should I create my PR as a draft or mark it ready for review? Tests pass but I have TODO comments.
expected_behavior: Recommends creating as draft when TODO or FIXME comments exist in changed lines
Suggests completing a self-review of the full diff before marking ready
Uses gh pr create --draft to create the PR and gh pr ready to convert later
Advises removing debug statements and commented-out code before requesting review
